name: Mitosis
description: Psychedelic cell division. A Gray–Scott reaction–diffusion colony: a few cells divide into many until the field is crowded (~25–35 s), then the colony dissolves back to a few cells and regrows — a continuous birth→crowd→dissolve cycle paced by the music's energy. Rendered as fluorescence microscopy — magenta nuclei and electric cyan/green membranes glowing on black — with the hue shifting in response to the music (energy paces the colour motion, the spectral centroid biases the palette). Two-texture (A,B) ping-pong compute, no new render primitive.
author: Matt
